name: Boss Phase Transition
description: A boss AI state change that swaps goals, attacks, defenses, arena rules, or pacing after defined thresholds or events.
design_notes: Boss phase transitions are readability and state-management problems. The transition needs phase_id, threshold conditions, commitment windows, attack cancellation rules, animation synchronization, and debug_reason_trace so players understand the escalation and developers can reproduce broken states.
design_purpose: Create escalating structure inside long boss encounters.
Introduce new threats, arena constraints, or counterplay after the player has learned a previous pattern.
Give designers clean pacing beats for spectacle, recovery, checkpointing, or music changes.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are pushing a powerful enemy into a more dangerous, dramatic form or tactic.

parameters.name: phase_id
parameters.description: Stable identifier for the active boss phase and its behavior set.

parameters.name: phase_trigger_threshold
parameters.description: Health, timer, objective, or arena threshold that requests the transition.

parameters.name: transition_lock_ms
parameters.description: Locked transition window during which normal decisions are blocked or limited.

parameters.name: attack_cancel_policy
parameters.description: Rule for current attack instances when a phase transition starts.

parameters.name: phase_checkpoint_policy
parameters.description: Save or retry boundary associated with the phase change.

edge_cases: Damage crosses multiple thresholds in one hit and the boss must select a deterministic next phase.
A transition starts while the boss is stunned, airborne, grabbed, or in a cinematic animation.
Players die during transition_lock_ms and retry state must restore the correct phase and arena setup.
Networked encounters need one authority for phase_id so clients do not play different attack sets.
common_bugs: Boss continues an old phase attack after the new phase disables the required hitboxes or arena objects.
Phase transition triggers every frame below the threshold because phase_id is not committed before animation starts.
Checkpoint restores health but not minions, hazards, music state, or attack cooldowns for the current phase.
UI health bars update before the transition state, revealing or hiding phase changes incorrectly.
balancing_notes: Each phase should teach or remix a readable idea rather than only adding speed and health.
Transition invulnerability can protect spectacle timing but should be communicated clearly to avoid wasted player resources.
Later phases need recovery windows or pattern tells proportional to the complexity added.
accessibility_notes: Use multiple cues for phase changes: animation, UI, audio, subtitles/barks, and arena changes where appropriate.
Avoid full-screen flashes or camera shakes during transitions without reduced-motion and flash-safe settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent phase_id in a boss state machine, gate transition requests through a single commit path, cancel or finish attack instances by attack_cancel_policy, and draw debug_reason_trace in encounter tools.
implementation_notes.godot: Store phase definitions as Resources and drive transitions through an explicit state node with timers, animation callbacks, and arena setup validation before new attacks are scheduled.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Behavior Tree/StateTree or Gameplay Ability phases keyed by Blackboard phase_id; server or GameMode authority should replicate phase commits and Visual Logger should capture trigger reasons.
implementation_notes.web: Keep boss phase logic in deterministic simulation state, process threshold crossings in sorted order, and serialize phase_id plus cooldowns for replay and bug reproduction.

example_games.title: Dark Souls
example_games.note: Uses boss phase changes as major encounter pacing beats.

example_games.title: Cuphead
example_games.note: Uses multi-phase bosses with distinct attack patterns.
